segments.text: In the course of time, David inquired of the Lord.
Shall I go up to one of the towns of Judah?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: he asked.
Go up.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Where shall I go?
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: To Hebron
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: So David went up there with his two wives, Ahinoam of Jezreel and Abigail, the widow of Nabal of Carmel.
David also took the men who were with him, each with his family, and they settled in Hebron and its towns.
Then the men of Judah came to Hebron, and there they anointed David king over the tribe of Judah. When David was told that it was the men from Jabesh Gilead who had buried Saul,
he sent messengers to them to say to them,
The Lord bless you for showing this kindness to Saul your master by burying him.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: May the Lord now show you kindness and faithfulness, and I too will show you the same favor because you have done this.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Now then, be strong and brave, for Saul your master is dead, and the people of Judah have anointed me king over them.
segments.speaker: David

verses.title: War Between the Houses of David and Saul

segments.text: Meanwhile, Abner son of Ner, the commander of Saul's army, had taken Ish-Bosheth son of Saul and brought him over to Mahanaim.
He made him king over Gilead, Ashuri and Jezreel, and also over Ephraim, Benjamin and all Israel.
Ish-Bosheth son of Saul was forty years old when he became king over Israel, and he reigned two years. The tribe of Judah, however, remained loyal to David.
Then Abner said to Joab,
Let's have some of the young men get up and fight hand to hand in front of us.
segments.speaker: Abner

segments.text: All right, let them do it.
segments.speaker: Joab

segments.text: Abner looked behind him and asked,
Is that you, Asahel?
segments.speaker: Abner

segments.text: It is
segments.speaker: Asahel

segments.text: Then Abner said to him,
Turn aside to the right or to the left; take on one of the young men and strip him of his weapons.
segments.speaker: Abner

segments.text: But Asahel would not stop chasing him.
Again Abner warned Asahel,
Stop chasing me! Why should I strike you down? How could I look your brother Joab in the face?
segments.speaker: Abner

segments.text: Abner called out to Joab,
Must the sword devour forever? Don't you realize that this will end in bitterness? How long before you order your men to stop pursuing their fellow Israelites?
segments.speaker: Abner

segments.text: Joab answered,
As surely as God lives, if you had not spoken, the men would have continued pursuing them until morning.
segments.speaker: Joab
